entities.name: ABOVEGROUND BIOMASS
entities.description: The total mass of living organic matter above the ground surface in a given area.

entities.name: CARBON STOCKS
entities.description: The amount of carbon stored in a reservoir, such as vegetation or soil, within a landscape.

entities.name: DEGRADED LANDSCAPES
entities.description: Land areas that have lost their biological or economic productivity due to human or natural factors.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns, often mitigated by carbon sequestration.

entities.name: NORTHWESTERN ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The geographical region where the study on biomass and carbon stocks was conducted.

entities.name: ALLOMETRIC EQUATIONS
entities.description: Mathematical models used to estimate biomass based on measurable plant dimensions like diameter and height.

entities.name: DIAMETER AT STUMP HEIGHT
entities.description: A measurement of the tree trunk diameter at 30 cm height used as a predictor for biomass.

entities.name: TREE HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical distance from the base of the tree to the highest point used as a predictor for biomass.

entities.name: STEM DENSITY
entities.description: The number of tree stems per unit area, which correlates with carbon stocks.

entities.name: BASAL AREA
entities.description: The cross-sectional area of tree stems at a specific height, which correlates with carbon stocks.

entities.name: LEUCAENA SP.
entities.description: A tree species that contributes significantly to the carbon stocks in the restored landscape.

entities.name: CALPURNIA AUREA
entities.description: A tree species that contributes significantly to the carbon stocks in the restored landscape.

entities.name: EUCLEA RACEMOSA
entities.description: A tree species that contributes significantly to the carbon stocks in the restored landscape.

entities.name: DODONAEA ANGUSTIFOLIA
entities.description: A tree species that contributes significantly to the carbon stocks in the restored landscape.

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide to mitigate climate change.

entities.name: RESTORATION PRACTICES
entities.description: Actions such as grazing exclosures implemented to recover degraded landscapes.
